story.title: Persist workspace (polygons + cameras, never image bytes) to localStorage with restore-on-load and Clear All
story.as_a: Frigate user refining zones across multiple sessions
story.i_want: my polygons and camera list to survive page refreshes and browser restarts, with a prompt to re-add the background image
story.so_that: an accidental refresh or returning tomorrow never costs me drawn work

acceptance_criteria: Given polygons and cameras exist, when the page is refreshed, then the full workspace (polygons with names/types/points, cameras, active selections) is restored and YAML regenerates identically to pre-refresh output

Given a restored workspace whose camera had an image, when the page loads, then the canvas shows a re-upload prompt naming the original file and its dimensions, and restored polygons render immediately once any image is provided
Given any Store mutation, when it completes, then the workspace is persisted within 500ms (debounced) and no image dataURL ever appears in the stored JSON
Given the user clicks 'Clear All Data', when confirmed via undo-toast pattern (instant clear + 5s undo), then localStorage key is removed and the app resets to first-run state
Given stored JSON with a missing or older schemaVersion, when load runs, then migration (or discard with a non-blocking warning) occurs and the app never crashes on corrupt storage
constraints: Single localStorage key 'fph.workspace.v2'; value is JSON.stringify of the workspace minus image bytes
Persist namespace owns all storage access: Persist.save(ws) (debounced 500ms), Persist.load(), Persist.clear(); no other code touches localStorage
Image bytes are never stored (master spec hard rule); imagesMeta {w,h,filename} only
Persist.save wraps setItem in try/catch — QuotaExceededError surfaces as a one-time warning banner, app keeps working unpersisted
Load failures (corrupt JSON, schema mismatch with no migration) discard storage and start fresh with a warning toast, never a crash
edge_cases: localStorage disabled (private mode in some browsers) — app runs normally, one-time 'persistence unavailable' notice
Two tabs open on the same file — last-writer-wins; no cross-tab sync in v0.2 (documented limitation)
Restore with polygons but user uploads a different-resolution image than original — normalized coords render correctly; meta dims shown in the prompt are informational only
Draft (unclosed) polygon at refresh time — draft points are persisted and restored mid-draw
risks: Low: quota exhaustion — impossible in practice once image bytes are excluded (workspaces are a few KB)
Medium: schema evolution across v0.2 specs (multi-camera changes shape) — mitigated by schemaVersion gate and migrate-or-discard policy

open_questions.question: Should the unclosed draft polygon persist across refresh?

options.label: Persist the draft (Recommended)
options.info: True 'don't lose work on refresh'; trivial since draft lives in the same state object.

options.label: Persist closed items only
options.info: Simpler mental model but loses in-progress polygons, the most annoying loss case.

tasks.description: Implement Persist namespace (save debounced/load/clear, schemaVersion gate, try/catch quota + corrupt handling) and subscribe Persist.save to Store
Implement restore-on-load: hydrate Store from Persist.load(), render re-upload prompt with stored filename/dims when imagesMeta exists
Add 'Clear All Data' button (instant + undo toast) wiping storage and resetting state
QA: refresh mid-draw, restart browser, corrupt the stored JSON by hand, disable storage, verify no image bytes in stored value

docs_md:
## Local Storage Persistence
The workspace auto-saves (debounced 500ms) to a single localStorage key after every change — polygons, cameras, and the in-progress draft, but never image pixels. On load, work is restored and the app prompts to re-upload the background image by name. 'Clear All Data' wipes everything with a 5s undo window.
metrics: Workspace restored with 100% field fidelity (deep-equal pre/post refresh, excluding image bytes) in QA scenarios
Stored value contains 0 occurrences of 'data:image' (grep on localStorage dump)
App load with corrupt/foreign stored JSON completes with 0 uncaught exceptions

decision_log.rationale: Single-key whole-workspace JSON is the simplest design that survives the multi-camera schema change (spec 08) — only the value shape evolves, gated by schemaVersion. Debounce prevents canvas-drag-time write storms once point editing (spec 05) lands.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: IndexedDB — could store image blobs, but master spec explicitly says don't store image bytes; IDB adds async complexity for no gain
Rejected: per-entity keys (one per polygon) — multi-key consistency problems, no benefit at KB scale
Rejected: storing image bytes anyway — quota risk and explicit master-spec prohibition
tradeoffs: Re-upload friction after restore is accepted by design
decision_log.confidence_rationale: Tiny serializable state, explicit master-spec rules, well-understood API.

defaults_applied: Assuming single-workspace persistence (no named saves/profiles) for v0.2
Assuming 500ms debounce is acceptable loss window on tab kill
Assuming 'Clear All Data' lives in the Controls card under Created Items
